test.command: set -e; ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then (cd "$ROOT/apps/mobile" && flutter test --concurrency=5); fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo test --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --jobs 5; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild test -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS'; fi

typecheck.command: ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then flutter analyze "$ROOT/apps/mobile"; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo check --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --all-targets; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS' build >/dev/null; fi

lint.command: set -e; ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then flutter analyze "$ROOT/apps/mobile"; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo fmt --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --all --check && cargo clippy --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS' build >/dev/null; fi

blockingIssues.description: Approval detail still cannot show the exact branch-switch target under the real bridge contract because the bridge continues to emit the generic `git.branch_switch` target identifier while the mobile fix/tests assume the DTO already carries the concrete branch name, so VAL-APPROVAL-002 remains unmet.

Notification preference toggles are still only local UI/persistence state and are not wired to actual approval/live-activity delivery or suppression behavior, so the earlier VAL-SETTINGS-004 failure remains unresolved.

The new reconnect/catch-up path still auto-retries only once because pairing, thread-detail, and thread-list controllers try to reschedule follow-up timers while their in-progress guards are still set, so longer outages still require a manual Retry tap despite the secure-store persistence fix.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/environment.md` to reflect that the mobile secure-store provider now uses `PersistedSecureStore()` rather than the old in-memory placeholder.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require integration workers to read the relevant `.factory/library/` contract/reference notes for the surfaces they touch, not only `user-testing.md`, before implementing cross-surface fixes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `integration-mobile-runtime-sync-and-safe-affordances-fix` found that the worker read `user-testing.md` but missed `.factory/library/api.md`, then implemented/tests-assumed an approval DTO shape that disagreed with the still-current bridge contract for branch-switch approval targets.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten the integration-worker handoff rules so required lifecycle/manual verification must either include recorded interactive observations or be explicitly called out as a procedure deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `integration-mobile-runtime-sync-and-safe-affordances-fix` found the worker marked `followedProcedure: true` even though the skill requires manual lifecycle verification and the handoff/transcript contained only automated command output with no `interactiveChecks` or equivalent recorded observations.
